Appvey 1.1.0+4bb877d

appvey v1.1.0+4bb877d
Copyright (c) 2024 Mohamed Hassan

Appvey cli for Appveyor CI/CD

Usage:
  Appvey [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  -v, --version   Show version information

Commands:
  project  Project command
  build    Build with more nested commands...
  http     Run Appveyor Rest Api Get/Post/Put/Delete.
           Example 1: Get request
           	appvey /api/projects/my-account/my-project/history?recordsNumber=20
           Example 2: Post request using json file
           	appvey -m post --json @file.json  /api/account/my-account/builds
  users    Users command
  config   Configure Appveyor token and account and allow storing token to Environment variables 
           	or Windows Credential Manager (Windows users only).
           Example 1: Storing token to Environment Variable
           	appvey config -a my-account -t my-token --action save
           Example 2: Storing token to Windows Credential Manager
           	appvey config -a my-account -t my-token --action save --use-cred
           Example 3: Show token store information
           	appvey config -a my-account --action info

cancel: Cancel build.
Example:
	appvey build cancel -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project 1.2.3

Usage:
  Appvey build cancel <build-version> [options]

Arguments:
  <build-version>  Build Version [required]

Options:
  -s, --slug                                     Project slug [required]
  -t, --token                                    Appveyor token v2.
                                                 To enter your token, you have four options:
                                                 • Enter your token from your keyboard.
                                                 • Type a hyphen ('-'): This allows you to directly type or paste your token from your keyboard.
                                                 • Type '@' followed by the filename: This tells the system to read your token from the specified file.
                                                 • Ignore this option: If you don't enter anything, the system will try to automatically read your token from an environment variable or Windows Credential Manager: Available for Windows users only. This assumes you have already set up the token using config command.
  -a, --account                                  Appveyor User account: Optional. If not provided, the system will look for your account in the environment variables. This assumes you have already set up the account using config command.
  -p, --pa, --proxy-address <http://proxy:port>  Proxy server should be in the form http://proxy:port
  -u, --proxy-user, --pu <username:password>     Proxy user/password should be in the form 'username:password'
  --save                                         File to save output response.
  -o, --output                                   File to save screen output.
  -w, --what-if, --wi                            Run the command without executing the actions of the command so no changes occur.
                                                 It displays optins and argument values and the expected Http Request:
                                                  (Url, Method  <Get|Post|Put|Delete>, JsonBody. [default: False]
  -v, --verbose                                  Verbose http connection details. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                 Show help and usage information

start: Start build with more nested commands...

Usage:
  Appvey build start [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  commit            Start build of specific branch commit.
                    Example:
                    	appvey build start commit -a my-account  -t my-token -s my-project -b master 856cf6c2
  pr, pull-request  Start build of Pull Request.
                    Example :
                    	appvey build start pr -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project 1234
  recent            Start build of branch most recent commit.
                    Example 1:
                    	appvey build start recent -s myproject --browse
                    Example 2 using environment variables with = separator:
                    	appvey build start recent -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project --browse  var1=value1 var2=value2
                    Example 3 using file:
                    	appvey build start recent -s myproject --browse @myenv.txt

	appvey project list -a my-account -t my-token

Usage:
  Appvey project list [options]

Options:
  -t, --token                                    Appveyor token v2.
                                                 To enter your token, you have four options:
                                                 • Enter your token from your keyboard.
                                                 • Type a hyphen ('-'): This allows you to directly type or paste your token from your keyboard.
                                                 • Type '@' followed by the filename: This tells the system to read your token from the specified file.
                                                 • Ignore this option: If you don't enter anything, the system will try to automatically read your token from an environment variable or Windows Credential Manager: Available for Windows users only. This assumes you have already set up the token using config command.
  -a, --account                                  Appveyor User account: Optional. If not provided, the system will look for your account in the environment variables. This assumes you have already set up the account using config command.
  -p, --pa, --proxy-address <http://proxy:port>  Proxy server should be in the form http://proxy:port
  -u, --proxy-user, --pu <username:password>     Proxy user/password should be in the form 'username:password'
  -s, --save                                     File to save output response.
  -o, --output                                   File to save screen output.
  -w, --what-if, --wi                            Run the command without executing the actions of the command so no changes occur.
                                                 It displays optins and argument values and the expected Http Request:
                                                  (Url, Method  <Get|Post|Put|Delete>, JsonBody. [default: False]
  -v, --verbose                                  Verbose http connection details. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                                 Show help and usage information
